symbols.name: isJackpotHit
symbols.detail: [USED] Runtime-imported by 1 file: src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No similar functions found in codebase | [OK] Logic matches all three documentation sources: count DIAMOND symbols across entire grid, return true when count >= 4. | [LEAN] Flat double-loop count against a single threshold. Exactly matches the documented 5×3 DIAMOND≥4 rule; no abstraction or generalization needed. | [NONE] No test file exists. Critical game logic (jackpot trigger at ≥4 diamonds) has zero coverage — no happy path, boundary (exactly 4 vs 3 diamonds), or edge cases (empty reels, all diamonds) tested. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc/TSDoc comment. Missing description of purpose, parameter shape (reels grid dimensions), return semantics, and the ≥4 DIAMOND threshold rule.

actions.description: Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol: `isJackpotHit`

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 9.5/10 (0 FAIL, 1 WARN, 16 PASS)

rules.detail: File is fully strict-compatible — explicit return type, typed parameters, no implicit any. Cannot verify tsconfig from file alone, but nothing in the code circumvents strict checks.
No explicit or implicit `any`. All types flow from the imported `Symbol` type.
String literal comparison `sym === "DIAMOND"` correctly narrows the `Symbol` union. No unsafe type assertions.
No complex object transformations; utility types (Pick/Omit/etc.) are not applicable to this function signature.
`ReadonlyArray<ReadonlyArray<Symbol>>` correctly marks both the outer reel array and each inner symbol array as immutable.
No new type/interface declarations in this file; only consumes the imported `Symbol` type.
No obvious lint violations. `let` for a mutable counter is appropriate; `for...of` loops are idiomatic.
`isJackpotHit` is a public export with no JSDoc comment. The docs describe its contract well, but the function itself carries no inline documentation.
`import type`, `ReadonlyArray`, and `for...of` are all idiomatic modern TypeScript. No deprecated APIs.
Single `import type` from a local module. No side-effect imports, no circular dependency risk, placement in `src/` matches project structure.
Pure synchronous function; no promises, no async I/O, no error handling needed.

Slot-machine domain confirmed by `.anatoly/docs/`. `isJackpotHit` is deterministic — it counts symbols in already-resolved reels, introduces no RNG of its own, and contains no `Math.random()`, hardcoded secrets, `eval`, or command injection. RNG certification concerns belong in `src/rng.ts`, not here.
Iterates a fixed 5×3 grid (max 15 cells). An early-exit once `diamondCount >= 4` is theoretically possible but the iteration budget is negligible and the current clarity is preferable.
Pure function with no side effects and no external dependencies. Trivially unit-testable by passing reel arrays.
No gaps: `satisfies`, `const` type params, and `using` have no natural application in this single-function utility. Existing code is already optimal.
Gambling domain confirmed. Jackpot detection is transparent and deterministic — a regulated-gaming audit requirement. No RNG, no floating-point money arithmetic, and no mutable shared state.

suggestions.description: Add JSDoc to document the jackpot threshold and the semantics of the return value for API consumers.
suggestions.after:
/**
 * Returns `true` when four or more DIAMOND symbols appear anywhere
 * across the 5×3 reel grid in a single spin.
 *
 * @param reels - Column-major reel grid (outer = reels, inner = rows).
 * @returns `true` if jackpot is triggered; caller is responsible for applying the prize.
 */
export function isJackpotHit(reels: ReadonlyArray<ReadonlyArray<Symbol>>): boolean {
